description: Engineered T7lac regulatory region: the bacteriophage T7 phi10 (class III) promoter immediately followed by a lac operator O1, giving LacI-controllable, low-basal expression from T7 RNA polymerase.

references.authors: D Imburgio, M Rong, K Ma, W T McAllister
references.title: Studies of promoter recognition and start site selection by T7 RNA polymerase using a comprehensive collection of promoter variants

references.authors: J W Dubendorff, F W Studier
references.title: Controlling basal expression in an inducible T7 expression system by blocking the target T7 promoter with lac repressor
The nucleotide sequence of the lac operator
references.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA

references.authors: M Lewis, G Chang, N C Horton, M A Kercher, H C Pace, M A Schumacher, R G Brennan, P Lu
references.title: Crystal structure of the lactose operon repressor and its complexes with DNA and inducer

label: T7lac
note: Engineered T7lac regulatory region: the bacteriophage T7 phi10 (class III) promoter immediately followed by a lac operator O1, giving LacI-controllable, low-basal expression from T7 RNA polymerase.

label: T7 promoter
note: Canonical bacteriophage T7 phi10 class III promoter consensus TAATACGACTCACTATAG (18 bp).

label: T7 RNAP binding domain
note: Upstream binding/recognition domain of the T7 promoter (TAATACGACTCAC, 13 bp, approx -17..-5), the functional analog of the -35/-10 region for the single-sub...

label: +1 transcription start
note: Class III T7 promoters initiate at +1 with a G/poly(G) run and a purine/GTP preference.

label: lac operator O1
note: Natural 21 bp lac operator O1, AATTGTGAGCGGATAACAATT (the standard T7lac/lacUV5 operator form; the central GCGG is contacted by the LacI hinge helices in the...
